title: Semantic HTML Rendering, Contextual Escaping, and Determinism · Language Clinic: Renderer Contract
summary: Text nodes can inject markup, code is double-escaped, attributes are unquoted, heading IDs collide, dictionary order leaks into output, and raw HTML bypasses the trust model. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the renderer contract increment in deterministic context-safe semantic HTML renderer. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory…
objectives: Render each validated node through one owned semantic mapping without reparsing generated strings as source.
Test mixed quote, list, code, paragraph, blank-line, and malformed nesting against tree and source-span invariants.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Predict Renderer Contract · Theory Renderer Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory publishing team handles case 9451 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to render every node as deterministic semantic HTML through context-safe boundaries; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Render each validated node through one owned semantic mapping without reparsing generated strings as source.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-html-rendering-escaping-3
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-html-rendering-escaping-3.
# Case 5798: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def worked_static_site_html_rendering_escaping_3(nodes: int = 14, rendered_nodes: int = 14, escaped_values: int = 6, unsafe_contexts: int = 0, duplicate_ids: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "readerworked3",
        "source5798",
        "parse3dekrow",
        "routeworked35798",
        "artifact5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if nodes < 1 or rendered_nodes < 0:
        return False, rendered_nodes, "render-count"
    if rendered_nodes != nodes:
        return False, rendered_nodes, "render-coverage"
    if escaped_values < 1:
        return False, escaped_values, "escaping-evidence"
    if unsafe_contexts > 0 or duplicate_ids > 0:
        return False, unsafe_contexts + duplicate_ids, "unsafe-html"
    assert rendered_nodes == nodes
    return True, rendered_nodes, "semantic-safe-html"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Build Renderer Contract evidence · Theory Renderer Contract
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-renderer-contract-n3dxs7" that uses case 9924 to render every node as deterministic semantic HTML through context-safe boundaries. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-renderer-contract-n3dxs7
# Competency: ssg-renderer-contract.
# Case 9924: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def evidence_ssg_renderer_contract_n3dxs7(nodes: int = 14, rendered_nodes: int = 14, escaped_values: int = 6, unsafe_contexts: int = 0, duplicate_ids: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_n3dxs7 = "n3dxs7-7sxd3n-9924"
    assert evidence_variant_n3dxs7
    evidence_axes_n3dxs7 = (
        "readern3dxs7",
        "source9924",
        "parse7sxd3n",
        "routen3dxs79924",
        "artifact9924n3dxs7",
        "recovery7sxd3n9924",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_n3dxs7)) == 6
    if nodes < 1 or rendered_nodes < 0:
        return False, rendered_nodes, "render-count"
    if rendered_nodes != nodes:
        return False, rendered_nodes, "render-coverage"
    if escaped_values < 1:
        return False, escaped_values, "escaping-evidence"
    if unsafe_contexts > 0 or duplicate_ids > 0:
        return False, unsafe_contexts + duplicate_ids, "unsafe-html"
    assert rendered_nodes == nodes
    return True, rendered_nodes, "semantic-safe-html"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Render each validated node through one owned semantic mapping without reparsing generated strings as source.
